questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: The Library of Alexandria was a great center of learning that was lost
questions.explanation: Covers the library's greatness AND its destruction.
questions.passageText:
Long ago, in ancient Egypt, the city of Alexandria was home to the most famous library in the world. Built more than 2,000 years ago, the Library of Alexandria held hundreds of thousands of scrolls — the books of that time.

Scholars from many countries traveled to Alexandria to study. They could read works on math, science, history, and poetry. Some say the library held copies of every book that existed.

Sadly, fires and wars destroyed the library over the centuries. Most of its scrolls were lost forever. Historians still wonder what knowledge disappeared with it.

questions.question: The ______ negotiator was able to settle the dispute peacefully.
What is the author's purpose?

wrongExplanations.0: Has facts but takes a side.

questions.passageText: Sugary soda has no place in schools. Each can contains 10 teaspoons of sugar. Studies show that students who drink soda gain weight, have more cavities, and feel less focused in class. We don't allow alcohol or candy bars in vending machines — why allow soda? Schools must ban soda for the health of our students.
questions.passageTitle: Soda Should Be Banned in Schools

questions.question:
Column A: Sides of an octagon
Column B: 8
BARK : DOG ::
questions.explanation: BARK is the sound a DOG makes. MEOW is the sound a CAT makes.

wrongExplanations.1: Feather is part of a bird — not a sound.
wrongExplanations.2: Fin is part of a fish.
wrongExplanations.3: Leaf is part of a tree.

questions.question: Andy has 3× more apples than Brad. If Brad has 6, Andy has?
Which sentence should come LAST in a paragraph?
choices: For example, exercise improves heart health.
In summary, exercise improves the body and mind.

questions.question: What is paragraph 2 mostly about?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 describes who visited and what they could read — the library's contents/users.
questions.passageText:
Long ago, in ancient Egypt, the city of Alexandria was home to the most famous library in the world. Built more than 2,000 years ago, the Library of Alexandria held hundreds of thousands of scrolls — the books of that time.

Scholars from many countries traveled to Alexandria to study. They could read works on math, science, history, and poetry. Some say the library held copies of every book that existed.

Sadly, fires and wars destroyed the library over the centuries. Most of its scrolls were lost forever. Historians still wonder what knowledge disappeared with it.

questions.question: Best heading for: snake, lizard, turtle, alligator

wrongExplanations.0: Reptiles aren't 'cold' as a defining trait — they're cold-blooded but not literally cold.

questions.question: Liam thinks watering his plant causes growth. What would WEAKEN his conclusion?
choices: Other plants in the garden also grew
Plants that weren't watered grew the same amount
questions.explanation: If unwatered plants grew JUST AS MUCH, watering didn't cause growth.

wrongExplanations.0: Sunlight could help too but doesn't disprove watering.
wrongExplanations.1: Other plants don't affect Liam's plant.

questions.question: MUSIC : COMPOSER ::
questions.explanation: COMPOSER creates MUSIC. AUTHOR creates STORY. Creator to creation.

questions.question: What is paragraph 3 about?
questions.passageText:
Coral reefs are sometimes called 'rainforests of the sea' because they support an incredible variety of ocean life. Though reefs cover less than 1% of the ocean floor, they are home to about 25% of all marine species.

Reefs are built by tiny animals called coral polyps. Over thousands of years, polyps build hard skeletons that grow into massive structures. Fish, turtles, sharks, and many other creatures live in and around these structures.

Today, coral reefs face serious problems. Warmer ocean temperatures cause coral 'bleaching,' which can kill the coral. Pollution and overfishing add to the threats. Many scientists worry that reefs may not survive without major changes.

questions.question: Which does NOT support: "Reading improves your writing"?
choices: You learn new vocabulary from books.
You see how good writers structure sentences.
Reading shows you how to use punctuation correctly.

questions.question: My friend invited my family and ______ to dinner.
If Sara studies at the library, she gets quiet time. Sara got quiet time. What can you conclude?
choices: She might have studied at the library
She didn't study at the library
questions.explanation: Library gives quiet time, but Sara could get quiet time other ways too.

questions.question: Plane flies 600 miles in 2 hours. Mph?
Paragraph 3 is about what?
choices: The 2019 photograph of a black hole
questions.passageText:
A black hole is a place in space where gravity is so strong that nothing — not even light — can escape. Once something falls in, it's gone forever.

Black holes form when massive stars run out of fuel and collapse on themselves. The star's matter gets crushed into a tiny point with infinite density. The space around it bends in strange ways.

For a long time, black holes were just a theory. Even Einstein wasn't sure they really existed. But in 2019, scientists captured the first photo of a black hole, proving they're real. The image showed a glowing ring of gas around a dark center — the 'shadow' of the black hole itself.

questions.question: Best topic for paragraph about reasons to recycle:
choices: Recycling saves resources, reduces pollution, and creates jobs.

questions.question: Which is OPINION, not fact?
choices: Monarchs are the most beautiful butterflies
questions.passageText:
Every fall, millions of monarch butterflies travel up to 3,000 miles from their summer homes to winter roosts in central Mexico.

Scientists have discovered that monarchs navigate using the sun's position and Earth's magnetic field. They have an internal compass in their antennae.

The monarch migration is now under threat. Logging in Mexico's forests has destroyed roosts. Herbicides have killed milkweed — the only plant where monarchs lay eggs. Conservation groups are working to protect forests and encourage planting milkweed.

questions.question: Which CANNOT be a rectangle?
questions.explanation: Trapezoid has 1 pair parallel; rect needs 2.

questions.question: The ______ desert had no ______ for miles.
Best concluder for paragraph about why kids should learn coding:
choices: Learning to code prepares kids for the jobs of tomorrow and teaches problem-solving.

questions.question: The ______ cat ______ the mouse for hours.
Although the evidence seemed ______, the detective remained ______ and continued investigating.

wrongExplanations.3: Hidden evidence isn't something to be skeptical of.

questions.question: The teacher and the students ______ excited.
Best heading for: noun, verb, adjective, adverb
questions.explanation: All four are PARTS OF SPEECH — the technical term.

questions.question: Best heading for: noisy, loud, deafening, booming

wrongExplanations.2: Subjective and not all hurt ears.

questions.question: Read: "Studying in groups has advantages. You can ask questions. ______. Group members motivate each other." Best fill?
The committee ______ its decision today.
questions.explanation: "Today" can be past — "made" announces completed action.

questions.question: FORGET : REMEMBER ::
questions.explanation: FORGET and REMEMBER are opposites. LOSE and FIND are opposites.

wrongExplanations.0: Leave and depart mean the same.
wrongExplanations.2: Sleep and dream go together but aren't opposites.
wrongExplanations.3: Drop and fall are similar actions.

questions.question: Which word does NOT belong with the others?
questions.explanation: First three are landforms by RELATIONSHIP TO WATER. Mountain is by ELEVATION.

wrongExplanations.0: Peninsula is mostly surrounded by water.
wrongExplanations.2: Continent is the biggest land between oceans.

questions.question:
Column A: 50% of 200
Column B: 25% of 400
All planets orbit the sun. Mars is a planet. Therefore:
choices: All things that orbit are planets
questions.explanation: All planets orbit. Mars is planet. Mars orbits.

questions.question: BICYCLE : ROAD ::
questions.explanation: BICYCLE travels on ROAD. BOAT travels on WATER. Vehicle to surface it travels on.

wrongExplanations.1: Station is where a train stops, not what it travels on.
wrongExplanations.2: Driver controls a car — different relationship.
wrongExplanations.3: Airport is where a plane takes off, not what it travels on.

questions.question: What is the author's purpose?
choices: Persuade readers to choose cats over dogs
questions.explanation: "Cats make better pets than dogs" + "should choose a cat" = persuade.
questions.passageText: Cats make better pets than dogs. They are quieter, cleaner, and don't need to be walked twice a day. Cats can be left home alone for a few days, while dogs cannot. They use a litter box, so you don't have to take them outside in the rain. Anyone who wants a low-maintenance pet should choose a cat.

questions.question: Which is the BEST topic sentence for a paragraph about taking care of plants?
choices: Caring for plants requires attention to water, light, and soil quality.
questions.explanation: Lists three care areas the paragraph can develop.

questions.question: All rectangles have four sides. All squares are rectangles. Therefore:
questions.explanation: Squares ARE rectangles. Rectangles have 4 sides. So squares have 4 sides.

wrongExplanations.0: Trapezoids have 4 sides but aren't rectangles.

questions.question: FREEZE : MELT ::
questions.explanation: FREEZE (water → ice) and MELT (ice → water) are opposite changes of state. STOP and START are opposite actions.

wrongExplanations.0: Heat and cool are also opposite states, but stop:start more directly captures the action reversal.
wrongExplanations.1: Build and break are different actions, not exact reverses.
wrongExplanations.3: Push and pull are opposites of force, not state.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: The Wrights owned a bike shop
The Wright brothers were the first to build a powered airplane
questions.explanation: The whole passage tells the story of their first powered flight.

wrongExplanations.3: TOO NARROW — true but doesn't capture the brothers.

questions.passageText:
Wilbur and Orville Wright owned a bicycle shop in Ohio in the late 1800s. But their real passion was something else: they dreamed of building a flying machine.

At the time, no one had ever built a powered airplane that could carry a person. Many tried and failed. The Wright brothers studied bird flight, designed wings, and built models. They tested everything carefully.

In December 1903, on a windy beach in North Carolina, their work paid off. Their airplane, the Wright Flyer, flew 120 feet in 12 seconds. It doesn't sound like much today, but it changed the world. From that small flight came the entire age of aviation.

questions.question: All triangles have three corners. This shape has three corners. What can you conclude?
choices: The shape might be a triangle
The shape is definitely not a triangle
questions.explanation: All triangles have 3 corners. But other shapes could have 3 corners. So MIGHT be triangle.

questions.question: The flu spread ______ through the school, infecting half the students in a week.

wrongExplanations.0: Half in a week is fast.

questions.question: All days have 24 hours. Tuesday is a day. Therefore:
questions.explanation: All days = 24 hours. Tuesday is a day. Tuesday = 24 hours.

questions.question: What is paragraph 3 mostly about?
questions.passageText:
Honeybees are one of the most important insects on Earth. They pollinate about one-third of all crops humans eat. Without bees, foods like apples, almonds, blueberries, and broccoli would be much harder to grow.

When a bee lands on a flower to drink nectar, pollen sticks to its body. As it flies to the next flower, it carries that pollen with it. This is what allows plants to make seeds.

Unfortunately, bee populations are dropping. Diseases, pesticides, and loss of wildflowers are all making it harder for bees to survive. Scientists are working to find solutions before it's too late.

questions.question: What does "a single, lonely note" symbolize?
questions.explanation: The single note in an empty house mirrors absence.
questions.passageText: Riley walked through the old house her grandmother had left her. Dust covered everything. The floorboards creaked. In the corner sat an old piano, missing a few keys. She sat down on the bench. Her grandmother had played this piano. Riley remembered being small, sitting beside her, watching her hands move across the keys. She pressed one key. It still made a sound — a single, lonely note that filled the empty house.

questions.question: Best heading for: apple, orange, mango, banana
The ______ bus arrived ______ on time.
questions.explanation: Crowded bus arrived right on time.

questions.question: Paragraph 3 is about what?
choices: The benefits and limits of wind energy
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 covers clean energy AND wind not blowing constantly.
questions.passageText:
Wind has been put to work by humans for thousands of years. Ancient sailors used wind to push boats across oceans. Farmers used windmills to grind grain.

Today, wind is used to make electricity. Tall towers called wind turbines have blades that spin in the wind. The spinning powers a generator that creates electricity. The electricity is sent through wires to homes and businesses.

Wind is a clean energy source — it doesn't pollute the air or water. But it has limits. Wind doesn't blow all the time. So we still need other energy sources to fill the gaps. Still, wind power is growing fast around the world.

questions.question: The ancient ruins were ______ by tourists and ______ by archaeologists who studied them carefully.
Best opening for paragraph about why kids should read fiction:
choices: Reading fiction develops empathy, expands vocabulary, and builds imagination.

questions.question: The ______ smell ______ the kitchen with delicious aromas.
What is paragraph 2 about?
choices: Their preparation and study before flying
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 covers their study and testing.
questions.passageText:
Wilbur and Orville Wright owned a bicycle shop in Ohio in the late 1800s. But their real passion was something else: they dreamed of building a flying machine.

At the time, no one had ever built a powered airplane that could carry a person. Many tried and failed. The Wright brothers studied bird flight, designed wings, and built models. They tested everything carefully.

In December 1903, on a windy beach in North Carolina, their work paid off. Their airplane, the Wright Flyer, flew 120 feet in 12 seconds. It doesn't sound like much today, but it changed the world. From that small flight came the entire age of aviation.

questions.question: The chef's ______ for cooking was clear in every dish she made.
COMPASS : DIRECTION ::
questions.explanation: COMPASS shows DIRECTION. CLOCK shows TIME. Tool to what it shows.

wrongExplanations.1: Map shows a country but is more general.
wrongExplanations.2: Mirror reflects a face — different relationship.
wrongExplanations.3: Window provides a view — passive, not measuring.

questions.question: Main idea: "Elizabeth Blackwell was the first woman doctor in the U.S." Which sentence BEST supports this?
choices: Blackwell lost her sight in one eye.
Blackwell graduated from Geneva Medical College in 1849.
Blackwell was born in Bristol in 1821.
questions.explanation: Graduating from medical college DIRECTLY supports being a doctor.

questions.question:
Which transition fits?
"Ice melts at 32°F. ______, water boils at 212°F."
Which word does NOT belong?
questions.explanation: Oak, pine, maple are TREES. Rose is a FLOWER.

questions.question: Which word does NOT belong?
questions.explanation: Acute, obtuse, right are TYPES OF ANGLES. Equal describes equality, not angle type.

questions.question: BANANA : PEEL ::
questions.explanation: BANANA has a PEEL you remove. EGG has a SHELL you remove. Outer covering you discard.

wrongExplanations.1: Core is the INSIDE of an apple, not outer covering.
wrongExplanations.3: Stem is the small handle, not the covering.

questions.question: FAMISHED : HUNGRY ::
questions.explanation: FAMISHED is extreme HUNGRY. FURIOUS is extreme ANNOYED. Strong to mild.

wrongExplanations.0: Tired and sleepy are similar — not strong vs. mild.
wrongExplanations.2: Happy and glad mean the same.
wrongExplanations.3: FREEZING is the extreme of COLD — order is flipped.

questions.question: The ______ wind made it impossible to keep his hat on.
What is the author's purpose?
questions.passageText: Schools should allow phones in class. Phones help students look up answers when they're stuck. They let parents reach students in emergencies. Banning phones treats students like they can't be trusted. Today's kids have grown up with phones — schools should adapt, not fight a losing battle.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: Volcanoes — how they work, their power, and their benefits
questions.explanation: Covers cause, range of eruptions, and benefits.
questions.passageText:
Volcanoes are mountains with a special feature: they can erupt. Deep underground, melted rock called magma builds up. When pressure gets too high, it bursts through the surface.

Volcanic eruptions can be small or huge. Some volcanoes leak lava slowly. Others explode with great force, sending ash, rocks, and gas miles into the air. The biggest eruptions can change weather around the world for years.

Despite their danger, volcanoes also do good. The cooled lava breaks down into rich soil that plants love. Many volcanic islands are some of the most fertile places on Earth.

questions.question: Best heading for: heart, lung, brain, kidney
questions.explanation: All four are INTERNAL ORGANS specifically.

wrongExplanations.0: Too broad — finger is body part.

questions.question: What does "Maya bit her lip" suggest?
questions.explanation: Biting lip + emotional moment = trying not to cry.

wrongExplanations.3: PARTIAL — but the emotional context points to crying.

questions.passageText: The moving truck was loaded. Maya stood in the empty front yard. Her best friend Lin walked up. Neither of them spoke for a long minute. "I'll text you every day," Lin finally said. "Me too," Maya whispered. Lin handed her something small wrapped in tissue paper. "For the trip." Maya unwrapped it slowly. It was a tiny stone — gray, smooth, ordinary. "From our park. Where we built that fort. So you have a piece of home." Maya bit her lip and pulled Lin into a hug.

questions.question:
Which transition fits?
"Cats can climb trees easily. ______, dogs usually cannot."
What can you infer about Mia?
choices: She's scared too but hiding it well
She thinks the storm is funny
questions.explanation: Telling a calming story while a storm rages = brave for her brother. She likely scared too but staying strong.
questions.passageText: The wind howled outside as the family huddled in the basement. Their flashlights cast moving shadows on the walls. Eight-year-old Theo squeezed his sister Mia's hand harder. Mia, eleven, knew Theo was scared. She didn't say anything. Instead, she started telling him a story — a silly one about a chicken who became a pirate. Theo laughed despite himself. Above, the storm raged on.

questions.question: Why did Mei tell her mom "the bus was running late"?
choices: She didn't want her mom to feel guilty
questions.passageText: Mei stared at the empty bus stop. 5:47 PM. The last bus was supposed to come at 5:30. She pulled out her phone — battery at 4%. Calling her mom would use the last of the battery, but walking home alone would take 45 minutes. Mei thought about how her mom had been working three jobs since Dad got laid off. Calling now meant Mom leaving work to pick her up — losing wages they couldn't afford to lose. Mei tightened her backpack strap and started walking, taking the route under the streetlights. By the time she reached her front door, her feet ached and her phone had died. "Why didn't you call?" her mom asked. Mei just smiled and said the bus was running late.

questions.question: What is the author's purpose?
questions.explanation: "Pick up a book" = call to action.
questions.passageText: Reading every day will change your life. It builds vocabulary. It improves focus. It makes you a better writer. Studies show that students who read just 20 minutes a day score significantly higher on tests. So put down the phone, pick up a book, and read! You'll be glad you did.

questions.question: Topic sentence for paragraph about why exercise improves grades:
choices: Exercise improves grades by boosting focus, sleep, and brain health.

questions.question: What is the author's purpose?
choices: Inform about how glass is made
Argue glass is the best material
questions.passageText: Glass is made by melting sand at extremely high temperatures — over 2,500°F. The melted sand is then cooled and shaped while still hot. Different ingredients change the glass: adding lead makes crystal; adding metals creates colored glass. Modern glass making uses giant furnaces that operate 24 hours a day.

questions.question: The ______ trader haggled until he got a ______ price.
questions.explanation: Savvy (clever) trader gets a low (good for buyer) price.

questions.question: Distance run 5 miles in 50 minutes. Mph?
